title: thysselinum palustre var. sylvestre
intro: Thysselinum palustre var. sylvestre, also known as wild lovage, is a perennial plant in the carrot family that grows in damp or wet environments. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve gas and bloating. The plant is also believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and acting as an antispasmodic agent, making it useful for treating conditions such as menstrual cramps and irritable bowel syndrome. The herbal preparations of wild lovage can be used in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
